content.text: Design Note — SM-1030: Vendor Admin - Add Save View button with Select View drop down

Mirror the Purchasing Module's view management pattern (TrackerBaseService) directly in Vendor Admin. Hide existing tab system (preserve code) and show all columns by default, then layer on Select View / Save View functionality.
— returns id=>name list, auto-creates Default if missing
— creates Default view with empty filters
— returns view list; if $full=1 returns full records with all config fields
— creates or updates a view (POST with name, filters, sort, displayColumns, columnWidth, pinColumns, optional view_id for update)
Left toolbar: mat-select dropdown for Select View (bound to
Right toolbar: Add Vendor, Clear Filter, Save View, Delete View (conditional on non-Default), Refresh, Export
{id, key, filters, sort, displayColumns, columnWidth, pinColumns}
— applies selected view config, saves view ID to localStorage
— restores grid state: filters, sort, visible columns, column widths, pinned columns
— opens SaveViewDialogComponent, captures current grid state (filters, sort, displayed columns, column widths, pinned columns), posts to backend, adds to viewsList, selects newly saved view
— opens ConfirmDialogComponent, calls backend delete, removes from viewsList, resets to Default
— resets to Default view, clears all filters/sort, shows all columns
Array of visible column field names as JSON
Object mapping colId to width as JSON
Object mapping colId to pinned direction as JSON
mat-select in left toolbar, loads from DB via getViews
Opens SaveViewDialogComponent, saves current grid config
Shown for non-Default views, soft-deletes via deleteview
All stored in vendor_admin_views table fields
Default view has empty config, applyViewConfig shows all
Last selected view ID stored in localStorage
SM-1030 Resolution: Add Save View / Select View to Vendor Admin
Added Select View dropdown (top-left toolbar) and Save View / Delete View buttons (top-right toolbar)
Saves and restores full grid state: filters, sort, column order, column widths, and pinned columns
Default view restores original column order from columnDefs
Delete View button is hidden when Default view is selected
Toolbar redesigned with flexbox layout, buttons aligned to far right
Unpinned Vendor ID and Company Name columns (user decides pinning via Save View)
New controller actions in vendor_admin_controller.php: getViews, saveview, deleteview
Fixed special character encoding in view names (e.g. & was saved as &)
The SQL migration must be run on test/prod environments to create the vendor_admin_views table: mysql -u root smuni < backend/app/config/sql/vendor_admin_views.sql
Navigate to Vendor Admin page - verify Select View dropdown shows "Default" view
Drag columns to reorder, apply filters/sort, then click Save View - enter a name and save
Switch to Default view - verify columns restore to original order with no filters
Switch back to saved view - verify column order, filters, sort, widths all restore correctly
Save a view with special characters in the name (e.g. "Flag & Status") - verify it displays correctly
Delete a custom view - verify it is removed from dropdown and grid resets to Default
Refresh the page - verify the last selected view is automatically restored
: Failed to load vendors. Please try again.

you may proceed. Done fixing. Reason: The vendor_admin_views table doesn't exist on the test server yet. This is the table used for the Save View / Select View feature (SM-1030). This is part of the sql migration and I have just added it.
dropdown shows "Default" view after navigating to the Vendor Admin page.
a new view can be saved successfully after dragging columns to reorder, applying filters/sort, and entering a name.
columns restore to their original order with no filters when switching back to the Default view.
the saved view restores column order, filters, sort, and widths correctly when reselected.
a view saved with special characters in the name (e.g., "Flag & Status") displays correctly in the dropdown.
a deleted custom view is removed from the dropdown and the grid resets to Default.
the last selected view is automatically restored after refreshing the page.
dropdown shows "Default" view after navigating to the Vendor Admin page.
a new view can be saved successfully after dragging columns to reorder, applying filters/sort, and entering a name.
columns restore to their original order with no filters when switching back to the Default view.
the saved view restores column order, filters, sort, and widths correctly when reselected.
a view saved with special characters in the name (e.g., "Flag & Status") displays correctly in the dropdown.
a deleted custom view is removed from the dropdown and the grid resets to Default.
the last selected view is automatically restored after refreshing the page.
Merged and Ready for Stage Testing\!
